feedback.text: I wholeheartedly believe that Digital Fairness Act should also take into consideration protecting the consumers of online digital goods such as video games as covered by "Stop Destroying Videogames" initiative. As well as looking towards into protecting the consumer's right and ability to pay for legal goods that otherwise could become (or already are) unavailable due to payment processors forbidding sale of what by them is considered unsafe. The video games affected by the shutdown of their online services more often than not simply become lost to time without intervention of consumers themselves, who are forced into a legal gray area of reverse engineering such products in order to restore their functionality. However, not every game can be saved this way which means anyone who has paid for the game simply loses their ability to play what they have paid for. Making sure that every future video game has measures in place regarding a future end of service would ensure these titles remain accessible no matter what. And when it comes to the right and ability to buy legal goods, consumers should not be forbidden from purchasing things that are otherwise legally allowed regardless of what payment processors or banks say. Not only this sets a bad precedent on what could potentially be taken down by these entities deeming "unsafe" but also could be used maliciously in future if left unregulated. Thank you for reading, time and consideration!
